raw_input:
[4.0/5] Good Results, Good Men's Lotion My husband has dry skin that is worsening now that cold weather has arrived and the heat is on in the house on pretty much a regular basis. I ordered this cream to help moisturize his dry skin as well as reduce his fine lines as he approaches the 'big 5-0'. He's not yet noticed a reduction to his fine lines though he hasn't been using the lotion very long but he is pleased with the job its doing to help moisturize his skin. His skin isn't nearly as dry as it had been so he is experiencing some much needed relief. He is a fan of the scent and he's happy that the lotion doesn't cause his skin to look or feel oily/greasy. He's definitely satisfied with this cream and the results he is receiving thus far. [4.0/5] Pricey but Decent This has worked pretty well for me over the past couple weeks of use, but I can’t say it’s worked better than products that cost half the price. It’s a good moisturizer, but it’s overpriced. [4.0/5] a face cream for use in the colder seasons I like that this product uses oils like jojoba, cocoa, and coconut. This face cream is a little on the richer side (but not super rich and thick to where it is hard to soak into the skin). It is a little on the oily side but the thing is now that the colder/fall/winter season is coming, this is the perfect face cream for the season. I would not recommend this for use during the hot summer but your skin will drink this up in the cold. The scent was light and pleasant, but that always comes down to personal preference as some people are very sensitive to smell.

It says this cream has collagen and stem cells, but I’m not sure how much of those ingredients are used in the formulation. It would be nice to know because if there is too little then there is probably very little anti wrinkle benefits. As with all beauty/skin care products it takes time to see the results. Overall this is a good face cream if you just want something moisturizing for the face but I can’t say yet if this will help erase or prevent any wrinkles. [1.0/5] If you sleep alone The marketing blurb says, among other things "this advanced man face care formula features stem cells to help revive the skin and smooth out imperfections".
Well... stem cells, if they were actually somehow harvested, in a cosmetics container, mixed with various other chemicals and ingredients would necessarily be dead and therefore completely irrelevant. And, of course, the stem cells would help address things such as diabetes and repair of heart muscle following heart attack, not zits on one's face. Not to mention that they would need to be live and 'human' cells. So, I am quite confident that the 'stem cells' claim is untrue and, even if it were true, the presence of dead stem cells in a cream would make zero difference.
Other than that, the cream is thick and, as other reviewer found, it has an aggressive smell that reminded me of Noxzema. So I would never use it during the day. At night, it would probably be Okay if used by men who sleep alone. [5.0/5] Leaves skin SO Soft, BUT Propylene Glycol is the 2nd ingredient (water is 1st)! This moisturizer leaves my boyfriend's skin so soft feeling. The scent is light and very likable! When the container arrives, it is in a box that looks really nice. After you unscrew the cap of the container, there is a plastic moisture cap that lays on top. That is good to keep on the container because it keeps keep moisture inside. It has a little finger handle on the top so it is easy to remove. It's a great product but it costs a bit too much as there are other products that can do the same for a lot less. [5.0/5] Moisturizes without a strong scent This works well on my face. I apply it around my face and neck and gently rub it in. It goes on a bit thick but rubs in nicely leaving my face smooth. The scent is mild and pleasant which is key for me since I don't like strong smells -- especially chemical smells or perfumes. Hoping that it works well to limit wrinkles as my faces ages -- we'll see. [4.0/5] Men's facial moisturizer with new ingredients! This facial moisturizer from Gentlehomme is a combination of effective ingredients for revitalizing, rejuvenating, and hydrating men's skin. Collagen and other herbal oils in this lotion are effective ingredients for reducing redness and long-lasting moisturizing. The cream has a medium thickness and absorption rate. It takes a while to be completely absorbed on my skin and it does not keep any residue on my face. However, it has a long-lasting effect which I can feel for a few hours. My skin becomes smoother when I use it once a day. The cream has a gentle scent. Although I feel some minor burning or stinging at the moment I apply the cream on my dry skin, the feeling fades away after a few minutes. I am also not sure about the impacts of stem cells on my skin yet, as I may need to use it for a longer time to see the promised positive effects on skin wrinkles and dark spots. Lastly, the cream is ordered by Gentelhomme (USA), but it is Made in China, which I was not expected initially, although I liked the quality and hydration performance on my skin. [5.0/5] Great Product that Works Face Moisturizers, and cosmetics in general, are typically not available or made with men in mind, so it is refreshing to find a beauty-type product and product line that caters to the male of the species.

Gentlehomme is certainly on to something pleasantly surprising, with a face moisturizer that contains many of the ingredients you might find in higher end products for women, such as collagen, oils, Vitamin E (tocopherol), butters, and stem cells. The ingredient list is rather exhaustive, with probably more than you need, but I have to appreciate a product that is well thought out. In terms of just the 'butters' and 'oils', the face moisturizer includes Cocoa Seed Butter, Coconut Oil, Avocado Oil, Bitter Orange Flower Oil, and Jojoba Seed Oil.

Unlike a few other reviews I've read of this product, I did not find the product to be greasy at all. It absorbed rather quickly into the skin, leaving it soft and supple, with a slight glow, without being shiny. It does have a thick, white, whipped consistency to it, similar to other facial creams out there, such as Noxzema. Also being four ounces, rather than two ounces, which is more standard for facial creams, makes this a pretty good value, overall.

Also unlike other comments that I've read, I did not find the Neroli scent overpowering or unlikeable at all. With that said, however, I found the smell to be very faint and more non-descript than of Neroli (Bitter Orange). The fragrance is very light--not necessarily masculine as one might expect, but almost indistinguishable from most other products out there--not offensive but not remarkable. My nose is rather sensitive to scents and I would have difficulty picking this one out in a crowd.

I like that I didn't have any negative reaction(s) to the product--no burning or stinging, rash or inflammation, and the moisturizer held up all day and all night without any discomfort or facial skin drying. It applies easily and a little of it can go a long way.

I would like to know a little bit more about the stem cells that are used in this formulation, as the literature and description is rather empty. Stem cells are supposed to help renew and rejuvenate the skin to make it look younger. Are these plant or fruit stem cells (most likely) or human stem cells? Live or extracts? Similar questions about the type of collagen that is used might also be beneficial, or worthy of a more detailed description or commentary.

It's advertised promise to moisturize, revitalize and hydrate seems spot-on. I rate this product as an excellent choice in the limited field of men's facial moisturizers.

Recommended. Made in China for Gentlehomme, LLC, U.S.A. Certified Vegan. [4.0/5] rich I like the smell. It says it’s Neroli essential oil. I would simply describe it as a midl cologne-like scent. The moisturizer is rich, creamy and silky. I would not describe it as a light moisturizer although it’s also not as heavy as some. However, I reserve it for situations that call for a strong moisturizer. It absorbs well and I like the container. [5.0/5] excellent hydration and not greasy I sometimes get a bit of dry skin on my face after a shower, if I have not used any moisturizer or facial lotion. It gets to the point of flaking in some areas, and needless to say I would like to avoid that. This cream is really nice. I have used many creams for my face over the years, but I think this is my favorite so far. It is thick and moisturizes without being greasy. It also saturates very well, and quickly. I hate creams that you need to work into the skin for a long time, and this one is not like that at all. I find that a little goes a long way, and so I just dab my finger in the jar, and put a few spots of cream on my face, then spread it around, and it seems like I hardly use any at all. Even though, the amount that I use, does the trick, and I have not had any dry skin. It comes in some pretty nice packaging, but most importantly it works very nicely, and delivers good results for daily moisturizing to avoid dry skin. I do recommend it! [5.0/5] This lotion is good for everybody This masculine smelling lotion is actually more feminine than you would think I've used some on myself men and women and nobody knows the difference in everybody likes it [4.0/5] Good & Hydrating [[VIDEOID:84a5ae506619190f739d3befcde0a9bc]] After about 2 weeks of use I can say this face cream has a nice texture and kept my skin moisturized throughout the day and doubles as a night cream. Be careful not to overdo it because a little goes a long way. If you use too much it feels greasy.
It's cruelty free and uses plant stem cells like some other expensive brands who use this technology. The only thing I didnt care for was the fragrance. Not a deal breaker but I personally just wasn't a fan. Although my wife said she didnt mind it and that it smelled masculine. All in all it's a good hydrating cream and is priced well. A great value, especially if not trying to break the bank on skincare. [5.0/5] The compact size ! I’m going to start with the scent so I understand it’s for your face but I wish the smell was a slight stronger other than that it’s a very good moisturizer and I would definitely recommend [5.0/5] Amazing Product!!!! I am absolutely OBSESSED with this product from Gentlehomme!!!! The scent is amazing and the moisturizing is out of this world!!!! 10/10 would recommend!!

response:
Product get mostly good reviews. Most buyers happy with moisturizing. Here what cave people say:

MOISTURIZING WORK GOOD. Many say skin feel soft, not dry no more. One man use for husband with dry skin in cold weather - husband happy. Another man say stop flaking after shower. Most agree little bit go long way.

SMELL MOSTLY GOOD. Most say scent light, mild, pleasant. Some say smell like cologne. One angry reviewer say smell too strong, like Noxzema. Scent called Neroli, from bitter orange flower.

PRICE TOO HIGH FOR SOME. Few reviewers say product work good but cost too much compared to cheaper products that do same thing.

NOT GREASY. Most say absorb well, no oily feeling left behind. One say use too much and get greasy, so use small amount.

STEM CELL CLAIM SUSPICIOUS. One very angry reviewer say stem cell claim nonsense - dead stem cells in cream do nothing. Other reviewers also wonder how much stem cell and collagen actually in product. Nobody sure if anti-wrinkle stuff work yet, need more time.

GOOD FOR COLD WEATHER. One reviewer say cream rich and oily, good for winter, bad for summer.

MADE IN CHINA. Some not expect this. Product sold by USA company Gentlehomme but made in China. Also certified vegan.
